follow_up.summary: Commit this repair and rerun the authoritative matrix with `--require-clean`.
Verify and close WorkUnit 0, then implement the shared workspace resolver and non-secret preferences layer.
Start FACMAN-WORKSPACE-PREFERENCES-AND-POLICY-01 and implement shared path resolution first.
Add schema-versioned non-secret preferences and allow the resolver to consume a reviewed preferred workspace after explicit environment precedence.
Prove the exact commit in the clean three-repository workspace, close WU1 evidence, then begin transactional instance lifecycle operations.
Prove the exact commit in the clean three-repository workspace, close WU1 evidence, then begin transactional instance lifecycle operations.

Start FACMAN-INSTANCE-LIFECYCLE-02 and keep destructive behavior backup-first and transaction-journaled.
Prove this exact revision in the clean three-repository workspace, close WU2 evidence, and begin portable deterministic snapshots and retention policy.
Prove this exact revision in the clean three-repository workspace, close WU2 evidence, and begin portable deterministic snapshots and retention policy.
Prove this exact revision in the clean three-repository workspace, close WU2 evidence, and begin portable deterministic snapshots and retention policy.

Prove this exact revision in the clean three-repository workspace, close WU2 evidence, and begin portable deterministic snapshots and retention policy.
Start portable deterministic snapshot create/list/inspect/verify/diff/restore and retention plan/apply work.
Run the clean three-repository repro matrix and attach its exact result to the workunit.
Run the clean three-repository repro matrix and attach its exact result to the workunit.
Start FACMAN-PROFILES-AND-TEMPLATES-02 from the closed snapshot baseline.
Run the clean three-repository repro matrix and close the exact R3.7 profile/template workunit.
Run the clean three-repository repro matrix and close the exact R3.7 profile/template workunit.
Start FACMAN-LOCAL-MOD-INVENTORY-02 from the closed customization baseline.
Use these stable inventory identities as the evidence input for the deterministic modset solver workunit.
Start FACMAN-LOCAL-MODSET-SOLVER-01 using the committed inventory identities as local evidence.
Reproduce this exact commit from a clean three-repository workspace and close FACMAN-LOCAL-MODSET-SOLVER-01.
Start FACMAN-SAVE-INDEX-AND-RETENTION-02.
Reproduce this exact commit from a clean three-repository workspace and close FACMAN-SAVE-INDEX-AND-RETENTION-02.
Start FACMAN-SERVER-PLAN-02.
Implement grammar-generated operational UX in the next R3.7 work unit.
Start FACMAN-OPERATIONAL-UX-03 from the generated command grammar.
Prove the full reversible lifecycle across direct, stdio, CLI, TUI, desktop, and package transports in WU10.
Execute FACMAN-R3.7-END-TO-END-LIFECYCLE-01 across supported transports and packages.
Land the independent cross-transport journey and performance proof.
Reproduce both implementation and proof commits from a clean three-repository workspace.
Rerun the complete clean three-repository reproduction from a fresh short build root.
Execute the R3.7 closeout target matrix and preserve human acceptance as a separate operator verdict.
Require the exact-sha GitHub Actions matrix to pass before freezing the R3.7 checkpoint.
Require exact-head CI, CodeQL, package, sanitizer, fuzzer, coverage, and AppKit compile results to pass.
Require exact-head Linux, macOS, sanitizer, fuzzer, package, coverage, AppKit, and CodeQL lanes to pass before closeout.
Require exact-head hosted Linux, macOS, Windows, sanitizer, fuzzer, package, coverage, AppKit, and CodeQL completion.
Require the exact workflow commit to pass Linux Debug/Release, sanitizers, fuzzers, coverage, packages, and all other target jobs.

highlights.subject: feat(preferences): add non-secret transactional product settings
highlights.summary: secret-like fields, unsafe paths, linked targets, future schemas, unavailable transports, and incomplete journal transitions fail closed.

highlights.subject: feat(instances): add reversible transactional lifecycle
highlights.summary: link/reparse crossings, multiply linked files, active run locks, uncertain transactions, hash drift, clobber targets, and malformed archives fail closed.

highlights.subject: feat(snapshots): add portable instance lifecycle
highlights.summary: exclude secrets and local paths and verify archive closure before restore.

highlights.subject: feat(profiles): add declarative customization lifecycle
highlights.summary: reserved Factorio arguments and all execution authority remain controlled outside customization documents.

highlights.subject: feat(workspace): centralize safe default resolution
highlights.summary: shared cross-frontend workspace resolution with legacy preservation and structured refusal.

highlights.subject: feat(preferences): add non-secret transactional product settings
highlights.summary: non-secret user preferences with reviewable plan/apply/reset commands and shared preferred-workspace resolution.

highlights.subject: feat(instances): add reversible transactional lifecycle
highlights.summary: reversible instance inspection, verification, diff, clone, display rename, owned-trash archive, and verified restore.

highlights.subject: feat(snapshots): add portable instance lifecycle
highlights.summary: portable instance snapshot management and reversible retention.

highlights.subject: feat(profiles): add declarative customization lifecycle
highlights.summary: declarative instance templates and portable launch-profile lifecycle management.

highlights.subject: feat(mods): add stable local inventory
highlights.summary: stable, local-only mod inventory and verification commands for R3.7.

highlights.subject: feat(modsets): add bounded local solver
highlights.summary: deterministic bounded local dependency plans and transactional modset apply and rollback.

highlights.subject: feat(saves): add structural index and retention
highlights.summary: bounded save indexing, sidecar association, drift detection, structural diff, and reversible retention.

highlights.subject: feat(servers): add non-executing plan bundles
highlights.summary: secret-free dedicated-server planning and portable export bundles.

highlights.subject: feat(frontends): generate guided operational forms
highlights.summary: generated guided operational forms and cross-frontend result visualizations.

highlights.subject: test(lifecycle): prove R3.7 cross-transport journey
highlights.summary: Cross-transport lifecycle, adversarial, and release-scale R3.7 proof coverage.

highlights.subject: ci(linux): add explicit Release proof
highlights.summary: Required Linux Release build and CTest evidence to the cross-platform closeout matrix.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): establish the R3.7 authority and evidence graph
highlights.summary: R3.7 now has target-local resumable governance and unambiguous baseline evidence.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): close local mod inventory workunit
highlights.summary: R3.7 wave state now records local mod inventory as closed and verified.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): close local modset solver workunit
highlights.summary: R3.7 wave state now records the local modset solver as closed and verified.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): close save intelligence workunit
highlights.summary: R3.7 wave state now records structural save intelligence as closed and verified.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): close server planning workunit
highlights.summary: R3.7 wave state now records server planning as closed.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): close operational UX workunit
highlights.summary: R3.7 wave state now records operational UX as closed.

highlights.subject: chore(aide): close R3.7 lifecycle proof
highlights.summary: R3.7 wave state now records WU10 as closed with advisory-only evidence.

highlights.subject: test(repro): bind package proof to the clean TUI build
highlights.summary: clean workspace proof now packages only artifacts produced by its own TUI-enabled build.

highlights.subject: feat(preferences): add non-secret transactional product settings
highlights.summary: affected-test module execution now exposes repository and tests helper import roots.

highlights.subject: feat(instances): add reversible transactional lifecycle
highlights.summary: affected native script tests now build real prerequisites and Python tests honor the requested Debug or Release artifact.

highlights.subject: fix(lifecycle): close cross-workunit integrity gaps
highlights.summary: Cross-workunit lifecycle correctness and generated-client compatibility defects found by R3.7 end-to-end proof.

highlights.subject: test(transaction): clean native smoke workspace
highlights.summary: Native transaction smoke no longer leaves an empty source-tree directory after passing.

highlights.subject: fix(portability): satisfy GCC and AppleClang warnings
highlights.summary: Linux and macOS warnings-as-errors portability failures in preferences and modset planning.

highlights.subject: fix(portability): initialize launch profile defaults
highlights.summary: Linux and macOS launch-reference compilation no longer depends on omitted aggregate fields.

highlights.subject: test(tui): initialize complete invocation shape
highlights.summary: Cross-platform TUI smoke compilation now matches the complete generated-client invocation shape.

highlights.subject: fix(profiles): archive directories atomically on POSIX
highlights.summary: Profile archive now uses a directory-safe atomic no-clobber move on POSIX.

highlights.subject: feat(instances): add reversible transactional lifecycle
highlights.summary: define immutable IDs, volatile exclusions, archive metadata, recovery states, and the explicit absence of purge.

highlights.subject: feat(preferences): add non-secret transactional product settings
highlights.summary: native transaction smoke, CLI read-only/secret rejection tests, contract goldens, schemas, frontend parity, full Release package proof.

highlights.subject: feat(instances): add reversible transactional lifecycle
highlights.summary: live schema validation, complete happy path, alternate-ID restore, run-lock refusal, and clone/archive/restore fault matrices.

risk_summary.summary: The stronger matrix builds one additional frontend target; no product behavior, authority, or test requirement changes.
This commit changes development truth only; it does not enable execution, setup mutation, networking, credentials, signing, or publication.
Metadata only; H1 and all execution, network, setup, credential, signing, and publication quarantines remain unchanged.
Users without an explicit workspace and without safe platform user paths now receive a refusal instead of a relative current-directory workspace; this is the intended safety correction.
AppKit catalog generation is contract-checked on Windows but native AppKit compilation remains a macOS CI responsibility.
AppKit catalog generation is contract-checked on Windows but native AppKit compilation remains a macOS CI responsibility.
AppKit catalog generation is contract-checked on Windows but native AppKit compilation remains a macOS CI responsibility.
AppKit catalog generation is contract-checked on Windows but native AppKit compilation remains a macOS CI responsibility.
None; this commit records verified task state only.
Snapshot references in instances.diff remain explicitly unavailable until WU3 creates the portable snapshot contract.
Snapshot references in instances.diff remain explicitly unavailable until WU3 creates the portable snapshot contract.
Snapshot references in instances.diff remain explicitly unavailable until WU3 creates the portable snapshot contract.
Snapshot references in instances.diff remain explicitly unavailable until WU3 creates the portable snapshot contract.
Snapshot references in instances.diff remain explicitly unavailable until WU3 creates the portable snapshot contract.
None; this commit records verified AIDE task state only.
Snapshot archives intentionally carry mod lock references only, never credentials or full secret-bearing configuration.
Snapshot archives intentionally carry mod lock references only, never credentials or full secret-bearing configuration.
Human acceptance remains separate and no execution, publication, signing, or promotion authority was granted.
Headless and benchmark values are planning inputs only; this commit does not enable process execution.
Headless and benchmark values are planning inputs only; this commit does not enable process execution.
Process execution and human acceptance remain separate, unpromoted authorities.
Archive metadata parsing remains intentionally bounded and does not claim portal availability or installation readiness.
This records machine-verifiable evidence only and does not grant portal, publication, or human acceptance claims.
Optional dependencies are not silently installed; they constrain the plan only when already requested or selected.
This ledger records machine evidence only and does not grant real Factorio, setup, network, signing, publication, or human-acceptance authority.
Deep Factorio save metadata remains explicitly unsupported.
This ledger records machine evidence only and does not claim deep save semantics, real Factorio execution, publication, or human acceptance.
LOW: execution remains unavailable and exports exclude saves unless explicitly requested.
LOW: this commit changes report-only AIDE state and no product behavior.
LOW: frontend controls submit the same generated request fields and backend validation remains authoritative.
LOW: this commit changes report-only AIDE state and no product behavior.
Restore and retention paths perform additional validation and may refuse previously ambiguous or corrupted state.
The full-scale corpus is intentionally opt-in because it creates a large temporary fixture and takes roughly two minutes on the validated Windows host.
Test-only path construction changed; transaction behavior and product runtime are unchanged.
No product behavior changes; this commit contains generated AIDE state only.
The solver construction and timeout acceptance behavior are unchanged.
Values are identical to the existing field defaults; product behavior and ABI layout are unchanged.
Test-only initialization changed; TUI runtime behavior is unchanged.
The target remains same-filesystem owned trash; cross-volume directory moves still fail closed.
Hosted Linux runtime increases because the native core is built once more in Release mode.
